 {"id":"bd-j20xh","title":"Glyph Width Calibration + Terminal Capability Policy","description":"Background
- Unicode glyph widths vary by terminal; miscalibration causes border drift.
- We need a centralized policy for Unicode vs ASCII fallback and width assumptions.

Scope
- Define capability checks for Unicode line drawing, emoji, and double-width glyphs.
- Detect terminal hyperlink capability (OSC 8) and expose in profile.
- Calibrate grapheme width expectations for diagram glyphs (incl. emoji, box drawing, arrows).
- Provide fallback rules for unsupported or ambiguous-width glyphs (ASCII set).
- Expose policy decisions in debug/evidence logs.
- Add manual override for capability flags via env/config.

Considerations
- Deterministic behavior; no runtime probing that changes output mid-session.
- Cache capability profile to avoid drift.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for width mapping and fallback selection.
- PTY E2E tests for ASCII vs Unicode modes.

Success Criteria
- Renderer picks correct glyph set per capability profile.
- No border misalignment for supported terminals.","notes":"BlueAnchor 2026-02-05: wired GlyphPolicy to TerminalCapabilities::with_overrides; added glyph capability overrides (FTUI_GLYPH_LINE_DRAWING/ARROWS/DOUBLE_WIDTH) + double_width field + width calibration gating for line-drawing/arrow glyphs; emoji now gated by caps unless env override; text_width CJK env override respects FTUI_GLYPH_DOUBLE_WIDTH in ftui-core + ftui-render; added unit tests for caps/override behavior. Clippy fix: replaced useless format! in mermaid_layout legend_entry_truncation. Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:31.634888273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:25.799101543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:25.799081536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":846,"issue_id":"bd-j20xh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GreenCove review: All acceptance criteria verified complete. GlyphPolicy implemented with 6 capability fields, detect()/from_env_with(), 6 env overrides, glyph_width() calibration, glyphs_fit_narrow() validation, 8 unit tests passing. OSC 8 hyperlink capability in terminal_capabilities.rs with extensive tests. Closing to unblock downstream chain (bd-12o4t, bd-12d5s, mermaid tasks).","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:24Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-j9itj","title":"Optimize markdown math conversion allocation hotspot","description":"Profiling run tests/artifacts/perf/20260424T161752Z-profile-sweep found repeated markdown math conversion as the top actionable optimization target. heaptrack_profile_sweep_off_direct_report.txt reports 6,189,740 allocation calls through unicodeit::latex_to_unicode in ftui-extras/src/markdown.rs; perf_profile_sweep_off_report.txt shows StrSearcher::new / str::replace at 7.21% of cycles. Implement as an extreme-software-optimization one-lever pass: deterministic bounded memoization/precompilation for latex_to_unicode output, golden Text equivalence tests for inline/display/fallback math, no global unbounded cache, then rerun profile_sweep and markdown tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:43:18.526534553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:49:37.424732138Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:49:37.423614074Z","close_reason":"Implemented bounded renderer-scoped markdown math memoization, persistent demo renderers, performance artifacts, and green workspace verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jbg","title":"Implement forms and picker widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: FORMS AND PICKERS

## Background
Interactive forms and picker dialogs are common UI patterns for configuration, data entry, and selection. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Form Widget
- Field types: TextInput, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Number
- Validation support with error messages
- Tab navigation between fields
- Submit/Cancel actions
- Vertical or horizontal layout
- Required field indicators

### Form API

pub struct Form {
    fields: Vec<FormField>,
    focused: usize,
    on_submit: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&FormData)>>,
}

pub enum FormField {
    Text { label: String, value: String, placeholder: Option<String> },
    Checkbox { label: String, checked: bool },
    Radio { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Select { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Number { label: String, value: i64, min: Option<i64>, max: Option<i64> },
}

impl Form {
    pub fn new(fields: Vec<FormField>) -> Self;
    pub fn focus_next(&mut self);
    pub fn focus_prev(&mut self);
    pub fn submit(&self) -> FormData;
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<ValidationError>;
}

### Picker Widgets
- FilePicker: Browse filesystem with filtering
- ColorPicker: Visual color selection
- DatePicker: Calendar-based date selection
- ConfirmDialog: Yes/No confirmation

### Picker API

pub struct FilePicker {
    current_dir: PathBuf,
    filter: Option<String>,  // glob pattern
    show_hidden: bool,
    selection: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl FilePicker {
    pub fn new(start_dir: impl AsRef<Path>) -> Self;
    pub fn filter(mut self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn show_hidden(mut self, show: bool) -> Self;
    pub fn selected(&self) -> Option<&Path>;
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] Form field navigation works
- [ ] Validation errors display correctly
- [ ] Submit collects all values
- [ ] FilePicker navigates directories
- [ ] ColorPicker returns valid RGB

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All form field types implemented
- [ ] Keyboard navigation complete
- [ ] FilePicker with glob filtering
- [ ] ConfirmDialog with Yes/No
- [ ] Accessible focus indicators

@@ -0,0 +1,59 @@
+# Markdown Math Cache Optimization Report
+
+Run ID: `20260424T180619Z-math-cache`
+
+## Summary
+
+This pass optimized the markdown math conversion hotspot identified in
+`tests/artifacts/perf/20260424T161752Z-profile-sweep/optimization_handoff.md`.
+
+The final implementation keeps LaTeX conversion memoization bounded and scoped:
+
+- `MarkdownRenderer` owns a 128-entry FIFO cache keyed by exact LaTeX source.
+- Inline math, display math, and math-flavored code blocks all use the same cached path.
+- Demo markdown screens now keep persistent themed renderers, cloning them only to apply per-frame width and table animation options.
+- The rich text screen uses separate persistent renderers for static markdown and streaming markdown so streaming fragments do not evict the static panel's hot math entries.
+
+## Before vs Final
+
+Baseline artifact: `../20260424T161752Z-profile-sweep/profile_sweep_off_cycles200.json`
+
+Final artifact: `profile_sweep_after_split_cycles200.json`
+
+| Metric | Before | Final | Change |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|
+| elapsed_ms | 7905.631 | 5404.941 | -31.63% |
+| renders_per_sec | 2276.858 | 3330.286 | +46.27% |
+| total allocations | 28476383 | 12620357 | -55.68% |
+| p99 allocations/frame | 41719 | 7247 | -82.63% |
+| total allocated bytes | 7622989884 | 7257076386 | -4.80% |
+| p99 frame time | 4018 us | 1830 us | -54.45% |
+| presenter p99 | 132 us | 94 us | -28.79% |
+
+Hyperfine comparison, 80-cycle profile_sweep:
+
+| Artifact | Mean | Min | Max |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|
+| `../20260424T161752Z-profile-sweep/hyperfine_off_1.json` | 2.693 s | 2.574 s | 2.871 s |
+| `hyperfine_after_split_cycles80.json` | 2.266 s | 2.170 s | 2.380 s |
+
+Mean wall time improved by 15.86% in the 5-run hyperfine comparison.
+
+## Heaptrack Result
+
+Baseline direct heaptrack reported:
+
+- `heaptrack_profile_sweep_off_direct_stderr.txt`: 11751058 allocation calls.
+- `hotspot_table_by_count.md`: 6189740 allocation calls through the `unicodeit::latex_to_unicode` replacement stack in `ftui-extras/src/markdown.rs`.
+
+Final direct heaptrack reported:
+
+- `heaptrack_after_split_direct_stderr.txt`: 5622122 allocation calls.
+- `heaptrack_after_split_direct_report.txt`: the largest remaining `latex_to_unicode` replacement stack is 25304 calls.
+- `heaptrack_after_split_direct_report.txt`: no residual `markdown_live_editor` stack appears in the LaTeX conversion search; remaining LaTeX calls are from the rich-text streaming path.
+
+## Notes
+
+- `profile_sweep_after_cycles200.json` records the first renderer-scoped cache attempt. It was rejected because the profile got slower: the markdown screen built fresh renderers every frame, so the cache lifetime was too short.
+- `profile_sweep_after_persistent_cycles200.json` and `profile_sweep_after_all_cycles200.json` are intermediate checkpoints before the final rich-text cache split.
+- `heaptrack_after_persistent.zst.zst` tracked `taskset`, not the profiling child process, and is intentionally ignored for conclusions. The useful heaptrack artifacts are the `*_direct*` files.
